CHANGE RECORD 
 
When the quality of the products changes, the QuID is updated, and a row is added to this table.   
The third column specifies which sections or sub-sections have been updated.  The fourth column should 
mention the reason for updating the document . Column 5 should include the names of the author (s) 
doing the changes. Including the name in column 6 shows that the document has been validated by the 
TAC/MFC PQ leader or deputy (or TAC/MFC PQ leader or the deputy). This validation means th e 
document was checked for contents, formats and consistency with other documents.

KEY DEFINITIONS 
Precision Measure of the number true positive predicted samples over the total 
amount of samples labelled as positive. 
Recall Quantifies the proportion of true positive predicted samples  among the 
total amount of positive samples contained in the reference 
Accuracy Indication of the proportion of samples that were correctly classified (as 
positive or negative) among the total amount of samples. 
Normalized Floating 
Algae Index (NFAI) 
 
The Normalized Floating Algae Index is a normalized version of the 
Floating Algae Index (Hu, 2009) defined by Stum et al. 2019. It is a remote 
sensing index providing information on the presence of sargassum. The 
normalization allows to limit the effects inherent to wide scale remote 
sensing monitoring such as different sensors, atmospheric  or oceanic 
conditions.  
Flag Normalized Floating 
Algae Index 
This flag variable describes the validity of the NFAI observation within 
each pixel of the dataset. The values indicate whether the pixel was 
covered by the day’s satellite images or masked because it is outside the 
images available, affected by cloud cover or on land. It also provides 
information on whether the pixel is classified as sargassum-free ocean or 
as a valid sargassum observation.  
Maximum Chlorophyll 
Index (MCI) 
The Maximum Chlorophyll Index  is a remote sensing index providing 
information on the presence of sargassum . It was defined by Gower et 
al., 2006, to highlight the presence of sargassum and chlorophyllous 
algaes.  
Flag Maximum 
Chlorophyll Index 
This flag variable describes the validity of the MCI observation within 
each pixel of the dataset. The values indicate whether the pixel was 
covered by the day’s satellite images or masked because it is outside the 
images available, affected by cloud cover  or on land. It also provides 
information on whether the pixel is classified as sargassum-free ocean or 
as a valid sargassum observation.

catalog_entry.summary: This document is a Quality Information Document (QUID) for the MULTIOBS_GLO_BGC_SURFACE_NRT_015_016 product, detailing its creation and updates by contributors from CMEMS. It includes an acronym table, key definitions of precision, recall, accuracy, NFAI, MCI, FAIA, and flag variables related to these indices.
